signals.title: EUR/USD — Trump Tariff Escalation
signals.text: Trump tariff escalation on EU autos sets up downside pressure toward 1.16 — EUR COT short -18.3k reinforces the bias. The mechanism driving this move is the potential trade war escalation, which could weaken the euro. The catalyst for this setup is the next ECB meeting on 30 Apr, where the market bias is towards a hold, but any change in stance could impact the euro.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike Bias
signals.text: AUD COT long +48.3k (74% long) creates crowded-long unwind risk — AUD/USD closed 0.7208 Friday; RBA hike bias (0% priced) sustains the bid but any disappointment triggers the unwind. The mechanism driving this move is the potential unwind of long positions due to a crowded trade. The catalyst for this setup is the next RBA meeting on 5 May, where a hike or hold will determine the direction of the Australian dollar.

signals.title: JPY/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Yield Advantage
signals.text: JPY 1W avg: +1.081% (n=7) outperforms USD 1W avg: -0.872% (n=7) with a G8 spread: 1.953%, setting up a bullish theme for USD/JPY — USD/JPY closed 157.03 Friday. The mechanism driving this move is the strong performance of the Japanese yen against the US dollar. The catalyst for this setup is the next BoJ meeting, where any change in monetary policy could impact the yen.

The current yield curve regime is a bull steepener, indicating recession pricing and risk-off sentiment — 2s10s spread: +49.8bp. The mechanism driving this move is the market's expectation of a potential recession. The catalyst for this setup is the upcoming economic data releases, which could confirm or deny the recession fears.
signals.title: USD — Yield Curve Regime
NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD at 0.5898, the -150bp carry differential favors the USD, but specs are short -16.8k contracts, which sets up a short-squeeze risk as the RBNZ's forward bias points to a hike, with 40% of a hike priced in. This divergence creates risk of a sharp unwind, likely to pressure the NZD/USD lower. The next RBNZ meeting on 27 May is a key catalyst as the key catalyst.

signals.title: USD/JPY — COT vs OIS Divergence + BoJ
signals.text: With the USD/JPY carry differential at +300bp, favoring the USD, and the pair currently trading at 157.03, specs short −88.5k sets up a notable divergence with the BoJ's implicit hike bias, where a 40% hike is priced in. This creates a short-squeeze risk, as the market is positioned against the direction the BoJ is signaling. The unwind of these positions is likely to be catalyzed by the next BoJ meeting on 28 Apr.
